title: South Shore school employee pleads guilty to stealing food, equipment for ‘Snack Shack’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Patrick Van Cott, former director of food services for Plymouth Public Schools, pled guilty to stealing food and equipment.
Van Cott used funds from schools and USDA to supply his 'Snack Shack' business in Barnstable.
He faces sentencing in August with potential penalties including up to 20 years in prison and $250,000 in fines.

title: New Allston hotel wins design award
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Atlas Hotel in Allston won the Lifestyle Hotel Public Space category at the 22nd Annual Hospitality Design Awards.
Located near Harvard's Enterprise Research Campus, the hotel features a lobby inspired by 'third places' concept.
The hotel includes amenities such as a fitness center, meeting spaces, and a rooftop bar called Foxglove Terrace.

title: JetBlue is ending service at Manchester-Boston Regional Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: JetBlue will end its service at Manchester-Boston Regional Airport on July 8 due to a capacity crisis.
MHT officials expressed disappointment, citing significant promotional efforts to support JetBlue service despite fuel price hikes.
JetBlue commenced service at MHT in January 2025 and has not provided immediate comment on the discontinuation.

title: What Boston meteorologists are saying about warmer temperatures into next week
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Boston's temperatures will rise from the 50s on Friday to the 70s and 80s over the weekend.
Meteorologists predict highs near 90°F in Boston on Tuesday, marking the hottest day of the week.
A jet stream build will bring a warm spell with temperatures above 80°F from Sunday to Wednesday.

title: France announces Boston-bound World Cup squad with sitcom-style video
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France's World Cup team will stay at the Four Seasons Boston and train at Bentley University.
France will play Senegal, Iraq, and Norway at MetLife Stadium, Lincoln Financial Field, and Gillette Stadium.
Notable absences from France's squad include Florian Thauvin, Randal Kolo Muani, and Eduardo Camavinga.

title: Cape Cod judge faces domestic violence charges
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Judge Robert A. Welsh III faces charges of assault and battery on an elderly household member.
Welsh's arraignment will occur at Brighton Municipal Court due to jurisdictional conflicts.
The judge is currently on administrative duties and not hearing cases during the legal proceedings.

title: 2026 summer movie preview: 26 must-watch films coming soon
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Two films filmed in Massachusetts are set for release: the Anthony Bourdain biopic 'Tony' and 'Super Troopers 3.'
Matt Damon stars as Odysseus in Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey,' part of the promising 2026 summer movie lineup.
'The Mandalorian and Grogu' film releases on May 22, following up on the Disney+ series, instead of a new season.

title: ‘If you earn an A, you deserve the A’: Readers react to Harvard’s proposed grade cap
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Harvard is considering capping A grades at 20% per class, allowing professors to award up to four additional A's.
A Boston.com informal poll found 58% of nearly 100 readers oppose a grading cap, while 36% support it.
Opponents argue grades should reflect mastery, while proponents claim grade inflation has devalued A's at Harvard.

title: New parking permits in South Shore town misspelled ‘parking’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Hull, MA officials are correcting parking permits due to a printing error that misspelled 'parking'.
Parking enforcement related to the new permits is paused until corrected versions are issued in June.
Residents can request free replacements of the incorrect permits while awaiting the corrected shipment.

title: Chargers troll Patriots over Mike Vrabel, Dianna Russini, and more in schedule release video
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Chargers used their schedule-release video to mock Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel and former NFL reporter Dianna Russini.
The Halo-themed video included references to the Patriots' Super Bowl loss and playful digs at Stefon Diggs and Cardi B.
The video also acknowledged the Patriots' previous playoff win over the Chargers with a subtle reference to the 16-3 victory score.
